title: Was Tojis mom a volunteer to be Evas 03 soul
display_title: Was Tojis mom a volunteer to be Evas 03 soul
wikitext:
[]: Was Toji's mom a volunteer to be Eva's 03 soul             Quote Post by Collossus » Mon Feb 17, 2014 8:24 pm 1 She was already dead when the series started 2. Toji's father and grandfather work at Nerv. 3. She was alive for a while after 2I (since she had another child after Toji). What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 17, 2014 8:58 pm There's been a theory for a while that ALL of class 2-A is nothing more than a pool to draw pilots from, inferring that the souls of all the children's mothers are somehow in Nerv's custody. Make of that what you will.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 17, 2014 10:06 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: There's been a theory for a while that ALL of class 2-A is nothing more than a pool to draw pilots from

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 17, 2014 10:39 pm How the heck would you keep a soul, anyway? Is it stated anywhere how they coax each Rei's soul into the next clone?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Feb 17, 2014 10:50 pm It is these problems that led me to conclude that only some Evas have souls, i.e. the ones that are explicitly confirmed to have them. The MPEs definitely don't. They don't need them- the MPE dummy plugs are explicitly labeled to be based on Kaworu, who can control an Eva without a "resident soul". The Eva program was designed to develop a pilotless Eva that doesn't need the umbilical cable. That's why the Mass Production Series has both qualities. (Though it's confusing why Unit 02 is the "first production model" when Unit 04 clearly had some experimental qualities... probably something Asuka latched onto to bloat her ego)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 17, 2014 11:30 pm How does anyone store a soul? Put it down to the mysterious science of metaphysical biology. What is metaphysical biology? Don't ask. Ritsuko in Episode 23 and the Classified Information both state that Evas have souls, and all the Angels have souls too. The harpies are no exception. The real question is whose souls they have, and that is not explained at all. Kaworu can control an Eva himself because they're based off his body. His dummy system just mimics his thought patterns (just like Rei's dummy system did that), making it a crude substitute for a soul for an Eva to synch with. They can't generate an ATF or do anything by themselves. There's no way that Kaworu's dummy system can do what Kaworu's soul can. As for the umbilical cable, that's just a power supply. (Though it's confusing why Unit 02 is the "first production model" when Unit 04 clearly had some experimental qualities... probably something Asuka latched onto to bloat her ego)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 17, 2014 11:37 pm Correct me if I'm wrong, Chuckman: Ursus, I think Chuckles is making a note about Unit-02's "title" of being the "First Production Model" as Asuka states it to be in Asuka Strikes! , and how it relates to how Unit-04 seems to be a somewhat experimental unit. (For my own thoughts, I'm not sure what's experimental about 04.) Notes: Screwed up unit denominations in both Chuckman's post and my own. Fudge. Consider post worthless.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 17, 2014 11:51 pm Yes, that's established fact. Eva-00 is the prototype, Eva-01 is the test type (grown from Lilith) and Eva-02 is the first production model. Eva-03 and Eva-04 were both production model Evangelions - the experimental thing was the installation of the S2 engine into Eva-04, which blew up Nerv's Nevada branch. The Eva itself wasn't experimental, the S2 engine was. (And it's hinted that Eva-04 blew up due to sabotage. The MP Evas, running off the same S2 engine, work just fine.)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:08 am Shit. I've been talking about Unit-04 all this time- I was supposed to be talking about Unit-03. Dammit. Post edited to reflect error.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 18, 2014 10:03 am Chuckman wrote: It is these problems that led me to conclude that only some Evas have souls, i.e. the ones that are explicitly confirmed to have them. The MPEs definitely don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:38 pm UrsusArctos wrote: How does anyone store a soul? Put it down to the mysterious science of metaphysical biology. What is metaphysical biology? Don't ask.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:09 pm An entire meat freezer full of frozen female corpses? Ick. I wouldn't have had trouble if Kaworu himself was piloting those MP Evas, because he would have been able to string-puppet the entire goddamn lot of them himself anyway. What I don't like is the idea that a Dummy Plug, a mere pastiche, should be able to achieve the same effect. That really goes against what Ritsuko said about a Dummy System in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:11 pm The dummy plug is a soul in the same way that the MAGI system is Ritsuko's mum. It just does a really good job of emulating the host material.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:16 pm UrsusArctos wrote: An entire meat freezer full of frozen female corpses? Ick.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:49 pm Kendrix wrote: My problem is, "how'd they gather the souls". In the cases we heard about, they needed halfway functional Evas for that, Evas that weren't built untill much later.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Feb 18, 2014 2:40 pm And yet, Class 2-A seems to be curiously devoid of mothers. There's gotta be something to that.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 3:12 pm I'm inclined to lean towards the idea that some kind of soul storage facility does exist, and that it's just not spoon-fed to us.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 18, 2014 3:23 pm Chuckman wrote: This is the problem with the notion of a cache of stored souls for Eva production. There are only two ways established in the show (other than natural death, obviously) to get a soul out of a body: in an Eva, or with an Anti-AT Field during an impact. Presumably the subject must be alive at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:34 pm Bagheera wrote: And yet, Class 2-A seems to be curiously devoid of mothers. There's gotta be something to that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:40 pm Chuckman wrote: Not having experienced maternal affection, they're more open to synchronization. Nerv could have purposely put all the kids with no mom because of accidents, natural death, and Second Impact in one class just as easily, if not more easily, than sucked out all their souls.
